rulings.Case Title: NORTH STAR CAPITAL ACQ LLC VS. SYLVIA PERKINS
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Hearing On Claim Of Exemption; Ntc Of Oppos
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar on Monday, March 17, 2014, Line 1, ASSIGNEE LVNV FUNDING LLC's Notice Of Hearing On Sylvia Perkin's Claim Of Exemption. The motion is denied. Defendant failed to carry her burden of proof to show that the money being held by the sheriff is exempt. See CCP 703.580(b). The levying officer is directed to release any earnings held to the judgment creditor for payment on the judgment, and thereafter $100 per pay period for payment on account of the debt to be garnished from wages. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: PAUL RYAN ASSOCIATES, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. 44 NORMANDIE, LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar on Monday, March 17, 2014, Line 2, CROSS DEFENDANT UNITED CALIFORNIA GLASS & DOOR's Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement. Granted. (CCP § 877.6.) Galvanknight Sheet Metal and Al's Complete Welding have not met their initial burden to show that the settlement was not made in good faith, (City of Grand Terrace v. Superior Court (1987) 192 Cal.App.3d 1251, 1261.) The settlement between United California Glass & Door ("UCGD") and Paul Ryan Associates is within ballpark based on the UCGD's proportionate liability for the construction defects associated with the Hoffman units and the replacement and repair costs. The declaration of Lydia V. Ko is insufficient to substantiate any evidence of collusion or the amount of Stearman fees. (Ko Decl., 11, 12, 15.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: RANDALL RADFORD VS. BAE SYSTEMS SAN FRANCISCO SHIP REPAIR, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Consolidate Actions For Trial Pursuant To Ccp 1048(A)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar on Monday, March 17, 2014, Line 3, PLAINTIFF RANDALL RADFORD's Motion To Consolidate Actions For Trial Pursuant To Ccp 1048(A). The motion is denied. The cases do not share sufficient common facts or law to warrant consolidation. Further, granting consolidation and having the trial for all actions on May 19, 2014, would prejudice defendant because expert discovery has yet to occur in the Foster action. Consolidation could lead to juror confusion. This motion is also untimely; it could have been brought much earlier when the Radford action was remanded from the Court of Appeal. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: THOMAS GARRISON MD VS. MEDICAL BOARD OF CALIFORNIA
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Hearing On Petition For Writ Of Administrative Mandate
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar on Monday, March 17, 2014, Line 19, PETITIONER THOMAS GARRISON's Petition For Writ Of Administrative Mandate. The petition for administrative mandamus is denied. Petitioner fails to establish that the penalty of revocation was a clear abuse of discretion. In Lake v. Civil Service Comm. (1975) 47 Cal.App.3d 224, 228, the court explains: The propriety of a penalty imposed by an administrative agency is a matter vested in the discretion of the agency, and its decision may not be disturbed unless there has been a manifest abuse of discretion. [citations] In reviewing the penalty imposed by an administrative body, which is duly constituted to announce and enforce such penalties, neither a trial court nor an appellate court is free to substitute its own discretion as to the matter nor can the reviewing court interfere with the imposition of a penalty by an administrative tribunal because in the court's own evaluation of the circumstances the penalty appears to be too harsh. [citations] Such interference, in the light of the foregoing authorities, will only be sanctioned when there is an arbitrary, capricious or patently abusive exercise of discretion by the administrative agency. Petitioner fails to show that respondent acted arbitrarily given the specific circumstances of this case. The Board did not fail to take into account mitigating evidence. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)
